steps.title: Identify the opening move
steps.content: The speaker begins by reporting a charge against him: that he seeks to destroy institutions. He then answers it by saying he is neither for nor against institutions, distancing himself from the accusation rather than accepting it.

steps.title: Identify the closing move
steps.content: A invents an increasingly prevalent attitude. B invents regret and prediction. C invents confessed shortcomings and past achievements. The closing lines move from defense to ambition, announcing a broad goal: establishing the institution of comrade love everywhere in cities, fields, woods, and above ships. The answer is D.
